frames: The agent stands upright with both feet on the ground, its arms bent and held near its chest.
It begins to lean forward, lifting its right leg while swinging its left arm backward.
The agent leans further, bending its left knee and extending its right leg behind it.
Its torso is now significantly angled forward, with the right leg extended and left leg bent.
The agent's body is very low to the ground, almost horizontal, with both legs extended backward.
It is nearly parallel to the ground, with arms extended forward and legs stretched out behind.
The agent is fully horizontal, moving forward with its entire body stretched out in the air.
It continues to glide forward in a rigid, prone position, maintaining its horizontal posture.
